description_long: This is a single, vibrant red rose with a lush bloom composed of numerous overlapping petals, creating a full and layered appearance. The petals are velvety and slightly curled at the edges, forming a classic spiral pattern typical of hybrid tea roses. The flower is attached to a green stem, which is partially visible and supports several broad, ovate leaves with a slightly glossy surface and visible veins. The leaves are arranged alternately along the stem and exhibit a healthy green coloration, with some minor imperfections and natural texture. The overall structure is delicate, with the flower head being the most prominent feature, and the stem and leaves providing a natural base. This rose would commonly be found in urban parks, gardens, or as part of decorative landscaping along sidewalks and public spaces.
description: A lush, red rose with layered, velvety petals and broad green leaves on a slender stem. The flower is full and vibrant, with a classic spiral petal arrangement.
description_view_0: The rose bloom is prominent, showing the layered petals and a partial view of the stem and leaves beneath.
description_view_1: The view highlights the back of the rose, with more leaves visible and the petals appearing slightly less open.
description_view_2: This angle shows the side of the rose, with the bloom slightly turned and the leaves fanning out below.
description_view_3: A frontal view of the rose, displaying the full face of the bloom and the symmetrical arrangement of petals.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a spiral pattern, overlapping and layered concentrically around the center, creating a whorled and radial symmetry typical of rose flowers.
